chief_complaint: A 45-year-old man presented with recurrent black stools and vomiting blood.
history: The patient had a history of recurrent black stools and vomiting blood. Initially diagnosed with gastritis, his condition worsened despite treatment, leading to severe bleeding.
exam: Vital signs: Not recorded. Physical examination: Not recorded.
labs: Imaging showed a suspected stomach hematoma, but the cause was a pancreatic pseudocyst eroding the stomach wall and a gastric artery.
correct_diagnosis: Pancreatic pseudocyst causing gastric erosion and bleeding
differentials: Pancreatic pseudocyst causing gastric erosion and bleeding
correct_ranking: Pancreatic pseudocyst causing gastric erosion and bleeding
